diagrams.title: Daemon Process Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Shows the complete lifecycle from regular process to daemon: fork → parent exit → setsid → chdir → redirect → becomes daemon under init
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how processes transform into daemons and their relationship with init

diagrams.title: Double Fork Technique
diagrams.description: Illustrates the two-fork process: first fork creates child and parent exits, second fork prevents session leadership and terminal control
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing why double-fork prevents daemon from acquiring controlling terminal

diagrams.title: Daemon vs Regular Process
diagrams.description: Comparison showing regular process connected to terminal vs daemon detached and under init supervision
diagrams.purpose: Understanding key differences between daemon and regular processes
